summary: Buddy lookup + cross-account request bug verification. Created /app/backend/tests/test_buddy_lookup_bug.py with 16 targeted regression tests (registration isolation, cross-account request, input normalization for whitespace/newlines/zero-width-chars/case, self-targeting error wording, not-found wording, handle lookups). All 16 NEW tests pass + all 31 existing tests pass (47/47 total). Frontend E2E reproduced the full reported flow with two isolated browser contexts: register A → onboard → /connect → copy buddy id → register B in separate context → /connect → paste A's BTF id → Send butterfly → input clears, NO 'cannot connect with yourself' or 'not found' error → B sees incoming with A's display name → accepts → both land on home with world switcher (paired). Backend logs confirm '[buddy-request] from_user=... lookup=... -> found=...' is emitted for every request including normalization cases.
critical_code_review_comments: _find_user_by_identifier (routes_buddy.py) hardening is solid: strip + remove invisible/ZWSP/BOM + uppercase + regex fallback for buddy_id, and normalize_handle for handles. Verified resilient against trailing/leading whitespace, '\n', BOM (\ufeff), ZWSP (\u200b), lowercase, and mixed case inputs.
Logging at INFO level '[buddy-request] from_user=... lookup=... -> found=...' provides good traceability without leaking secrets. Confirmed present in /var/log/supervisor/backend.*.log.
Self-error message updated to friendlier 'That looks like your own Butterfly ID — try theirs instead.' — confirmed via API and via UI when self-targeting own buddy_id and own @handle.
Not-found message exact wording confirmed: 'No one with that handle or ID was found.' (status 404).
Minor (pre-existing, not new): handle uppercase silently auto-lowercased instead of rejected — documented in iteration_1 review.
Minor (pre-existing): /api/files/{path} does not enforce pair-membership — same note as iteration_1.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (full cross-account buddy flow verified end-to-end in two isolated browser contexts)

test_credentials: Tests create users dynamically with TEST_ prefix. Sample UI run: TEST_uia_<hex>@bf.io (UserAlpha, BTF-YV9K-K9U9) and TEST_uib_<hex>@bf.io (UserBravo, BTF-D4BG-WGNR) — successfully paired via UI.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates fresh TEST_-prefixed users. No reliance on seeded sun@bf.io/varun@bf.io accounts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug regression suite is /app/backend/tests/test_buddy_lookup_bug.py — re-run for any future buddy/auth refactor. To run all backend tests: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://buddy-sanctuary.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/ -v. The user-reported bug ('No one with that handle or ID was found' on account A + 'You cannot connect with yourself' on account B) could NOT be reproduced with current code — likely fixed by aggressive input normalization (strip BOM/ZWSP/whitespace + case-insensitive regex fallback). Original bug was almost certainly caused by hidden whitespace/ZWSP/BOM in pasted buddy IDs from a previous client (mobile keyboards, password managers, or rich-text clipboard often inject these).
rca_of_the_issue: Original bug RCA: When user pasted a buddy_id from clipboard, the source app (or OS clipboard) injected zero-width / non-breaking space / BOM characters or trailing newline. Old code did a literal exact-match Mongo query on buddy_id ({'buddy_id': identifier}), so the paste with polluted chars never matched any user → 404 'No one with that handle or ID was found'. For the reverse direction, the OTHER user's identifier could have matched accidentally to self if it was an empty/whitespace-only string that normalize_handle reduced to '' and an earlier handle lookup happened to match the requester (very unlikely; more plausibly two browser windows were sharing the same cookie on the same domain, so 'A' was actually still authenticated as 'B' when trying to send). Mitigation: routes_buddy._find_user_by_identifier now strips \u200b/\u200c/\u200d/\u2060/\ufeff/\u00a0, uppercases, and tries case-insensitive regex fallback. Plus, log line '[buddy-request] ... lookup=%r' uses %r repr so hidden chars are visible in logs (e.g., 'BTF-3LK3-H778   ' with trailing spaces shown literally). Verified by 16 regression tests across all polluted-input variants.
